articles.title: Periscope Equity Makes Strategic Investment in Amusement Connect Payments Platform
articles.summary: Private equity firm Periscope Equity has made a strategic investment in Amusement Connect, a specialty payments platform built specifically for the amusement industry. The deal signals growing investor confidence in purpose-built fintech solutions for FEC operators. This infusion of capital is likely to accelerate product development and market expansion for a platform already embedded in amusement venues.

articles.title: ROLLER Partners with Adyen to Offer Embedded Financing to 3,000+ Venues
articles.summary: Venue management software provider ROLLER has partnered with payments giant Adyen to offer fast, embedded financing through a Capital product to more than 3,000 venues on its platform. FEC operators using ROLLER could gain access to working capital without traditional loan processes, easing cash flow for expansions or equipment purchases. This is a significant fintech development for the attractions software space.

articles.title: Sacoa Installs Cashless Payment Technology at Virginia Pinstack FEC
articles.summary: Sacoa has completed a cashless technology installation at a Pinstack family entertainment center location in Virginia, expanding its footprint in the FEC market. Cashless and card-based payment systems continue to replace token and coin systems across the industry, improving revenue tracking and guest convenience. This deployment highlights the ongoing transition FEC operators are making toward fully integrated cashless ecosystems.

articles.title: Slick City Action Park Names First-Ever Chief Marketing Officer Amid 40-Park Expansion
articles.summary: Slick City Action Park, the indoor dry-slide concept with 40 locations open across North America and internationally, has appointed Josh Cole as its first Chief Marketing Officer. The hire signals a maturing brand infrastructure as the company scales rapidly. FEC operators and investors should note Slick City as a fast-moving competitor in the indoor active entertainment space.

articles.title: K1 Speed Opens Multi-Level Indoor Go-Kart Track in Thornton, Colorado
articles.summary: K1 Speed has opened a new indoor amusement center in Thornton, Colorado, featuring a multi-level go-kart track. The opening continues K1 Speed's national expansion of its electric indoor karting concept. Multi-level track designs are an increasingly popular way for FEC operators to maximize vertical space in urban and suburban locations.

articles.title: SisyFox Launches Versatile Pay-Per-Play System Supporting RFID, Coins, and Credits
articles.summary: German game studio SisyFox has unveiled a new pay-per-play system designed specifically for indoor leisure and FEC operators, supporting coin systems, RFID wristbands, and credit payments with ticket dispenser integration. The system allows venues to link physical park infrastructure directly to attractions for seamless player identification and monetization. This type of flexible, low-maintenance payment hardware is increasingly essential for FEC operators looking to modernize revenue capture.

articles.title: Six Flags Expands Flexible Membership Program to Six Additional Parks
articles.summary: Six Flags Entertainment Corporation is rolling out its Flexible Membership program to six more parks, offering guests multi-park access and value-driven pricing throughout the year. Gold Memberships now include access to a designated group of parks, making the pass more competitive with Universal and Disney annual pass structures. FEC operators should monitor how regional park membership models evolve, as they set guest expectations for value and frequency of visits.

articles.title: Urban Air Adventure Park Opens New Location in Salem, Oregon
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park has opened a new location in Salem, Oregon, continuing the trampoline and adventure park chain's aggressive national growth strategy. The opening was previewed with a tour for local media, highlighting the breadth of activities available. Urban Air remains one of the most active FEC franchise expanders in the country, with implications for local independent operators in new markets.

articles.title: Urban Air Expands Into Maryland with New Indoor Entertainment Location
articles.summary: Urban Air Adventure Park is entering the Maryland market with a new indoor entertainment launch, further extending its East Coast presence. The chain's continued geographic expansion underscores the demand for multi-activity indoor entertainment centers in suburban markets. Independent FEC operators in the region should be aware of the competitive dynamics this brings.

articles.title: Spokane Valley Family Entertainment Center Closes After Two Years in Business
articles.summary: A family entertainment center in Spokane Valley has permanently closed its doors after just two years of operation, according to local reports. The closure highlights the operational challenges many smaller FECs face, particularly in the critical early years. It serves as a reminder of the importance of robust business planning, location selection, and differentiated offerings in a competitive market.

articles.title: Five Iron Golf Opens Second Dubai Venue, Signals International Indoor Golf Expansion
articles.summary: Five Iron Golf, the indoor golf and entertainment concept, has opened its second venue in Dubai's Business Bay district. The brand's international growth into the Middle East reflects broader global demand for social sports entertainment as an FEC category. Indoor golf continues to expand as a premium offering that blends sport, technology, and hospitality.

articles.title: Chuck E. Cheese Partners with Asmodee for Spot It! Summer In-Venue Experience
articles.summary: Chuck E. Cheese has teamed up with board game publisher Asmodee to bring a Spot It!-branded summer experience to its locations. The partnership blends tabletop gaming IP with the FEC environment, offering a fresh programming angle to drive repeat visits. Brand collaborations like this are an increasingly popular strategy for FECs to add novelty without major capital investment.

articles.title: Indoor Entertainment Center Leases Former Sears Space at Sunrise Mall
articles.summary: An indoor entertainment center has signed a lease to occupy part of the former Sears anchor space at Sunrise Mall, continuing the trend of FECs filling large-format retail vacancies. Repurposing dead mall anchor space remains one of the primary real estate strategies for FEC expansion across the U.S. This deal is a strong indicator of the ongoing retail-to-entertainment conversion opportunity.

articles.title: Thunderzone Family Entertainment Opens New Location in Carrollton, Georgia
articles.summary: Thunderzone Family Entertainment has opened a new venue in Carrollton, Georgia, adding to the regional FEC landscape in the Southeast. New FEC openings in smaller markets reflect growing consumer demand for local entertainment options outside major metro areas. Operators expanding into secondary markets should take note of the brand's approach.

articles.title: Tenpin Bowling and Entertainment Centre Opens in Blackpool, UK
articles.summary: UK bowling and entertainment brand Tenpin has opened a new venue in Blackpool, expanding its national footprint. The opening adds bowling, arcade games, and other entertainment options to one of the UK's most visited leisure destinations. Tenpin's continued expansion reflects sustained demand for multi-activity FEC concepts in the UK market.

articles.title: NRG Adventure Park Advances Plans in Jacksonville's Southside Commons
articles.summary: NRG Adventure Park is moving forward with development in Jacksonville's Southside Commons mixed-use district, adding another active entertainment venue to the Florida market. The project represents the growing integration of adventure parks into mixed-use retail and entertainment developments. FEC developers should monitor how anchor entertainment tenants are being used to energize mixed-use projects.

articles.title: Madison's 'The Gallery' Entertainment District Set to Break Ground This Summer
articles.summary: A new entertainment district called 'The Gallery' in Madison is scheduled to break ground this summer, signaling continued investment in destination entertainment complexes in mid-sized U.S. cities. Entertainment districts that combine multiple FEC-style attractions under one roof continue to attract developer interest. The project could serve as a case study for operators considering anchor roles in similar mixed-use venues.

articles.title: dvLED vs. Projection for Attractions: A Practical Guide to Choosing the Right Display Technology
articles.summary: A detailed analysis from an industry CTO breaks down the real trade-offs between direct-view LED and projection systems for entertainment and simulation environments, covering contrast, geometry, acoustics, and total cost of ownership. For FEC operators investing in immersive experiences, VR zones, or large-format display attractions, the choice between these technologies has significant long-term financial and operational implications. The article cuts through marketing hype to offer a framework for making the right decision for specific venue conditions.

articles.title: Dellshire Resort Opens in Wisconsin Dells with Fire-Breathing Dragon as Centerpiece
articles.summary: Dellshire Resort has opened in Wisconsin Dells, featuring a large fire-breathing dragon as its signature attraction element in an already highly competitive tourism market. The resort's theatrical centerpiece is a strong example of how entertainment destinations use dramatic signature experiences to build brand identity and social media buzz. FEC designers and operators can draw lessons from the resort's approach to creating memorable, shareable moments.

articles.title: Play CENLA Family Entertainment Center in Louisiana Shuts Down
articles.summary: Play CENLA, a family entertainment center serving central Louisiana, has closed its doors permanently. The closure adds to a pattern of smaller FEC closures that highlight the operational and financial pressures facing venues in smaller markets. Industry stakeholders should consider what support structures, programming, and revenue diversification strategies can help similar venues remain viable.

articles.title: Kids Bowl Free Program Returns to Kentucky and Southern Indiana This Summer
articles.summary: Several bowling centers across Kentucky and Southern Indiana are participating in the Kids Bowl Free summer program, offering complimentary games for children to drive traffic during peak season. The program is a well-established marketing tool for bowling centers looking to build family loyalty and upsell food, beverages, and shoe rentals. FEC operators with bowling components should evaluate similar promotional programs to boost summer lane utilization.

articles.title: Menasha's Sabre Lanes Bowling Alley Permanently Closes After Long Run
articles.summary: Sabre Lanes in Menasha, Wisconsin has permanently closed, with the venue directing its remaining customers to a sister location in Appleton. The closure of a longstanding bowling center underscores the structural challenges facing standalone bowling facilities competing against full-service FEC complexes. It reflects a broader industry consolidation trend as bowling migrates toward multi-activity venue formats.

articles.title: Masti Zone Opens New Family Entertainment Location in Lucknow, India
articles.summary: Masti Zone, a growing Indian family entertainment brand, has opened a new location in Lucknow, expanding its presence in one of South Asia's fastest-growing leisure markets. The opening reflects surging demand for organized indoor family entertainment in Indian tier-2 cities. International FEC suppliers and franchisors should take note of India as an increasingly significant growth market.

articles.title: Ninja Kingdom Pop-Up Indoor Attraction Launches at Delhi Mall This Summer
articles.summary: Nexus Select CITYWALK has brought a Ninja Kingdom indoor entertainment experience to its Delhi mall location for the summer season, catering to families seeking active indoor play. Pop-up and seasonal FEC attractions within retail malls are gaining traction in Asian markets as a flexible, low-capital strategy for entertainment programming. This model offers lessons for North American and European mall operators looking to animate vacant or underperforming space.

articles.title: Enchanted Forest Water Safari Opens 70th Anniversary Season with New Attractions
articles.summary: Enchanted Forest Water Safari, New York's largest water theme park, is opening its 70th anniversary season on June 10 with new additions including the Paul Bunyan's Log Haul experience. Milestone anniversaries provide powerful marketing moments for regional attractions, and the park's longevity is a testament to consistent reinvestment in the guest experience. FEC operators can draw inspiration from how legacy venues balance nostalgia with fresh attractions.

articles.title: London-Born Social Entertainment Venue with Virtual Soccer and Interactive Games Coming to Paris
articles.summary: A London-based entertainment concept featuring foosball, virtual soccer, interactive games, cocktails, and fast food is expanding to Paris, bringing a social sports entertainment format to a major new European market. The venue concept blends competitive socializing with food and beverage, a format growing rapidly across the UK and Europe. FEC operators globally should monitor how social sports entertainment concepts are scaling internationally.
